name: ETH 1h VWAP Reclaim Rotation v1
edge_mechanism: intraday inventory gets stretched away from value, price reclaims session value, and rotates back toward medium balance in a choppy tape
thesis: ETH 1h is the best lane in the allowed basket for dense value-reclaim logic. In a low-confidence chop regime, the edge should come from fading failed short-term displacement after value is reclaimed, not from demanding multi-bar trend confirmation.
invalidation_condition: If value reclaims do not produce enough post-cost rotation toward balance, or if ETH 1h keeps trend-extending after reclaim instead of mean-reverting, the thesis is wrong.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Short-term price displacement away from value fails, VWAP is reclaimed, and price rotates back toward medium balance before the next directional auction starts.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: ETH 1h is currently chop-dominant and transition-heavy rather than trend-persistent. That environment tends to punish late extension entries and reward reclaim-to-balance rotations.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is a better fit than ETH 4h for this idea because the mechanism needs enough repetition to clear costs. 1h should generate materially denser reclaim events than 4h while still staying orderly.

indicator_role_map.EMA_55: medium balance target and structure exit anchor
indicator_role_map.RSI_14: stretch qualifier; avoids entering without actual inventory displacement
indicator_role_map.ADX_14: trend veto so we avoid fading real expansion

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim can occur inside true continuation rather than failed displacement, causing repeated stop-outs when trend reasserts immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is good but expectancy is weak, shorten the hold and reduce runner share before adding more filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Many modest winners, fewer large winners, edge driven by repeated reclaim-to-balance moves and disciplined time exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: Screen shows non-sparse participation, first target hit rate is healthy, and at least one cross-lane preserves the same reclaim-to-balance behavior.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or value reclaims that repeatedly fail to travel beyond costs.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low; stretch plus reclaim is materially denser than prior breakout ceremony

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for dense intraday reclaim rotations in current chop.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 1h produces repeatable reclaim-to-balance trades with credible density and positive post-cost behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h either becomes too trend-persistent or reclaims stay too shallow to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent rotations back toward EMA_55, with most profit coming from early partials and quick balance moves.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same mechanism survives slower structure without collapsing into 0-trade grammar.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still trades enough to preserve the reclaim logic structurally.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower frequency than 1h but same reclaim-to-balance dynamic.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism generalizes to another liquid major with slower but cleaner rotation behavior.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows fewer but cleaner reclaim rotations.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h is too inert for reclaim rotation in this grammar.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count than ETH 1h, cleaner path to medium balance.

long: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
